title: Living in Boulder vlog 5 - 30 - 2017: Momssen & Decline & Fall of the Roman Empire

enrichment.summary: Kyle shares a vlog update on his late-night work schedule and his passion project: organizing public-domain history books (Mommsen's History of Rome and Gibbon's Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire) on his website. He explains his educational philosophy of always understanding the 'whole' before the details, and describes his multi-level navigation system (books, chapters, subjects) to make dense historical texts more accessible and enjoyable.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Always start with the overview/whole picture before zooming into details
You should make practice enjoyable so you do it constantly rather than forcing yourself
Listing chapter subjects aloud helps you follow along and stay oriented in dense books
Extremely excited about them — considers organizing them on his website one of the most exciting things he's ever done

entities: Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire

claims.claim: Livy’s work on Rome is around thirty-three hundred pages long.

claims.subject: Theodore Mommsen
claims.claim: Theodore Mommsen’s book is thirty-two hundred pages long.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I organized Mommsen’s book on my website to align with my educational philosophy.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My educational philosophy is to look at the whole picture first before diving into details.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: In 10th grade history class, I struggled with understanding the Peloponnesian War because we didn’t start with the broader historical context.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I organize books into four dimensions: books, chapters, subjects within chapters, and paragraphs within subjects.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I list all the subjects of each chapter at the top of the chapter to help readers understand the context.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Understanding the overview of a book helps bring the story to life, similar to how visuals help in television.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I created a page on my website where you can have the computer read the entire book aloud.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The most important thing in learning or training is to make practicing fun and engaging.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I started working on 'The Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire,' which is divided into six volumes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I’m creating a master link page to connect every chapter of 'The Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire.'

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I’m excited about how fast my computer is at linking and organizing content.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I aim to finish organizing all my classical books on my website within the next couple of weeks.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: This project is one of the most exciting things I’ve ever done.
